thig12.1:1.2: always going down to the water
thig12.1:1.3: from fear of my mistresses’ beatings,
thig12.1:2.1: But you, brahman, what do you fear
thig12.1:2.2: that you’re always going down to the water
thig12.1:2.3:
with shivering limbs, feeling great cold?
The Brahman:
thig12.1:3.3: You’re asking one doing skillful kamma
thig12.1:4.1: Whoever, young or old, does evil kamma
thig12.1:4.4:
from evil kamma set free.
Puṇṇikā:
thig12.1:5.4: is from evil kamma set free’?
thig12.1:6.1: In that case, they’d all go to heaven:
thig12.1:6.4: & anything else that lives in the water.
thig12.1:7.6: be from evil kamma set free.
thig12.1:8.1: If these rivers could carry off
thig12.1:8.2: the evil kamma you’ve done in the past,
thig12.1:8.3: they’d carry off your merit as well,
thig12.1:8.4: and then you’d be completely deprived.
thig12.1:9.1: Whatever it is that you fear,
thig12.1:9.2: that you’re always going down to the water,
thig12.1:9.4:
Don’t let the cold hurt your skin.”
The Brahman:
thig12.1:10.1: I’ve been following the miserable path, good lady,
thig12.1:10.2: and now you’ve brought me back to the noble.
thig12.1:10.4:
I give you this robe for water-ablution.
Puṇṇikā:
thig12.1:12.1: then don’t do any evil kamma,
thig12.1:12.3: But if you do or will do
thig12.1:13.1: you’ll gain no freedom from pain,
thig12.1:13.2: even if you fly up & hurry away.
thig12.1:14.1: go to the Awakened One for refuge,
thig12.1:14.4:
That will lead to your liberation.
The Brahman:
thig12.1:15.1: I go to the Awakened One for refuge;
thig12.1:15.2: I go to the Dhamma & Saṅgha.
thig12.1:15.4: That will lead to my liberation. * * *
thig12.1:16.1: Before, I was a kinsman to Brahmā;
